item.title: You can now transfer your chats and personal information from other chatbots directly into Gemini
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google is launching "switching tools" that, just as it sounds, will make it easier for users of other chatbots to switch to Gemini.

item.title: Wikipedia cracks down on the use of AI in article writing
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The site, whose policies are subject to change, has struggled with the issue of AI-generated writing.

item.title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: It's only the latest of several side projects that the AI startup has ditched over the past week.

item.title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren want the Energy Information Administration to gather more details about how data centers use power — and how that affects the grid.

item.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

item.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

item.title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

item.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

item.title: Mistral releases a new open source model for speech generation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The model, which lets enterprises build voice agents for sales and customer engagement, puts Mistral in direct competition with the likes of ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and OpenAI.

item.title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?

item.title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

item.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

item.title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

item.title: Melania Trump wants a robot to homeschool your child
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The first lady sees AI and robotics playing a prominent role in the future of American education.

item.title: Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced companion legislation to halt construction on new data centers until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation.

item.title: Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

item.title: Reddit takes on the bots with new ‘human verification’ requirements for fishy behavior
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.

item.title: Harvey confirms $11B valuation: Sequoia triples down
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Investors like Sequoia, Andreessen Horowitz, Kleiner Perkins, and Elad Gil can't get enough of AI legal tech startup Harvey.

item.title: Granola raises $125M, hits $1.5B valuation as it expands from meeting notetaker to enterprise AI app
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Granola's valuation jumped from $250 million to $1.5 billion with this round, and it has added more support for AI agents after users previously complained.

item.title: Meta launches new initiative to support entrepreneurship, drive AI adoption
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said in a memo to staff that small businesses have always been a big part of the company's business model, and that while tens of millions of entrepreneurs already use its platforms to grow and connect with customers, the company wants to do more in the space.
